qa_pairs.question: What will be the default standard environment for all clusters after April 1, 2024, and how can users specify a different default environment?
qa_pairs.answer: After April 1, 2024, `StdEnv/2023` will be the default environment for all clusters. Users can specify their own default environment at any time by modifying the `$HOME/.modulerc` file. For example, running `echo "module-version StdEnv/2020 default" >> $HOME/.modulerc` will set the default to `StdEnv/2020`. The user must log out and log in again for this change to take effect.

qa_pairs.question: Is it necessary to reinstall or recompile custom code and installed packages when the standard environment (`StdEnv`) changes on the clusters?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, if you compile your own code or have installed R or Python packages, you should recompile your code or reinstall the packages you need with the newest version of the standard environment.

qa_pairs.question: Is it permissible to mix modules from different standard environments within a single job on the clusters, and which environment is recommended for new projects?
qa_pairs.answer: No, you should use a single environment for a given job, as trying to mix different environments can lead to unpredictable errors. For new projects or if you want to use a newer version of an application, `StdEnv/2023` is recommended, which can be activated by adding `module load StdEnv/2023` to your job scripts.
